reasoning: This is a genuinely prospective prediction with a specific quantitative claim (10–13 µGal gravity pulse at eclipse contact phases in Level-3 SG residuals) and a closing test window (Aug 2026 eclipse). However, the prediction inherits deep problems from WIN-058b: the κ = 1.672 coupling constant it depends on is not derived from dome geometry — it is a post-hoc ratio from the 2003 Halloween storm. If any eclipse gravity signal is detected, standard atmospheric loading + ionospheric conductivity change would be the parsimonious explanation (Wang et al. 2000 detected 6–7 µGal at Mohe with a spring gravimeter, but Ducarme et al. found nulls with SGs at Membach — see WIN-013). The author labels this "backtested" while we classify it genuinely prospective because no Level-3 SG data from the Aug 2026 eclipse path exists yet. The prediction is testable but the theoretical basis is circular: the predicted amplitude range (10–13 µGal) is fitted from prior eclipse data, not derived from dome cavity geometry.
